story.soThat: I don't have to write repetitive code and can maintain consistency across the application
story.tasks: - Task 1: Create custom decorators (AC: 7.2.1, 7.2.4, 7.2.5) - Subtask 1.1: Create src/common/decorators/ directory - Subtask 1.2: Implement @CurrentUser() decorator (extract user from ExecutionContext → request.user) - Subtask 1.3: Implement @Public() decorator (use SetMetadata to set IS_PUBLIC_KEY = true) - Subtask 1.4: Implement @ApiPaginatedResponse() decorator (composite decorator using applyDecorators()) - Subtask 1.5: Create index.ts barrel export for decorators - Task 2: Implement hash utilities (AC: 7.2.2) - Subtask 2.1: Create src/common/utils/ directory - Subtask 2.2: Implement hash.util.ts (hashPassword, comparePassword using bcrypt) - Subtask 2.3: Add type definitions and JSDoc comments - Task 3: Implement date utilities (AC: 7.2.2) - Subtask 3.1: Implement date.util.ts (formatDate, toUTC, addDays, diffInMs) - Subtask 3.2: Add type definitions and JSDoc comments - Task 4: Implement string utilities (AC: 7.2.2) - Subtask 4.1: Implement string.util.ts (slugify, truncate, capitalize, removeSpecialChars) - Subtask 4.2: Add type definitions and JSDoc comments - Task 5: Implement validation utilities (AC: 7.2.2) - Subtask 5.1: Implement validation.util.ts (isValidPhone, isStrongPassword, sanitizeInput) - Subtask 5.2: Add type definitions and JSDoc comments - Subtask 5.3: Create index.ts barrel export for utilities - Task 6: Write comprehensive unit tests (AC: 7.2.3) - Subtask 6.1: Create src/common/utils/__tests__/ directory - Subtask 6.2: Write hash.util.spec.ts - Subtask 6.3: Write date.util.spec.ts - Subtask 6.4: Write string.util.spec.ts - Subtask 6.5: Write validation.util.spec.ts - Subtask 6.6: Write decorator tests (current-user.decorator.spec.ts, public.decorator.spec.ts) - Subtask 6.7: Run coverage report and verify 100% coverage for utilities - Task 7: Integration testing with existing modules (AC: 7.2.4, 7.2.5) - Subtask 7.1: Test @CurrentUser() decorator in AuthController - Subtask 7.2: Test @Public() decorator on login endpoint - Subtask 7.3: Verify hash utilities work with existing AuthService

story-context.acceptanceCriteria:
AC-7.2.1: Decorators created in src/common/decorators/: - current-user.decorator.ts → @CurrentUser() extracts user from request.user - public.decorator.ts → @Public() sets IS_PUBLIC_KEY metadata - api-paginated-response.decorator.ts → @ApiPaginatedResponse() creates Swagger schema
AC-7.2.2: Utility files created in src/common/utils/: - hash.util.ts → hashPassword(), comparePassword() using bcrypt - date.util.ts → formatDate(), toUTC(), addDays(), diffInMs() - string.util.ts → slugify(), truncate(), capitalize(), removeSpecialChars() - validation.util.ts → isValidPhone(), isStrongPassword(), sanitizeInput()
AC-7.2.3: All utilities have 100% unit test coverage - Each utility function has comprehensive unit tests - Test files located in src/common/utils/__tests__/ - Coverage verified with npm run test:cov
AC-7.2.4: @CurrentUser() decorator successfully extracts authenticated user in controllers - Decorator extracts user from request.user (set by JwtStrategy) - Works with existing JWT authentication from Epic 2 - Returns typed JwtPayload with userId, domainId
AC-7.2.5: @Public() decorator bypasses JwtAuthGuard when applied to routes - Sets IS_PUBLIC_KEY metadata to true - JwtAuthGuard checks for this metadata - Public routes (login, register, health checks) work without authentication

doc.title: Epic Technical Specification: Developer Infrastructure
doc.snippet: AC-7.2.1 through AC-7.2.5 define decorators (@CurrentUser, @Public, @ApiPaginatedResponse) and utility functions (hash, date, string, validation). All utilities require 100% test coverage. Security: bcrypt with 10+ rounds, XSS prevention, strong password validation.

doc.title: Epic Technical Specification: Developer Infrastructure
doc.snippet: @CurrentUser() decorator extracts authenticated user from ExecutionContext. @Public() decorator marks routes as public (bypass JWT guard). Utility functions: hash (bcrypt wrapper), date (formatting, timezone), string (slugify, truncate), validation (phone, password, XSS).

doc.title: Epic Technical Specification: Developer Infrastructure
doc.snippet: Decorator interfaces: CurrentUser extracts request.user (JwtPayload). Public sets IS_PUBLIC_KEY metadata. Utility function signatures: hashPassword(password, rounds), comparePassword(password, hash), formatDate(date, format), slugify(text), isValidPhone(phone), isStrongPassword(password), sanitizeInput(input).

doc.title: Epic Technical Specification: Developer Infrastructure
doc.snippet: Password hashing: bcrypt with minimum 10 rounds (BCRYPT_ROUNDS env). Timing-safe comparison: bcrypt.compare prevents timing attacks. XSS prevention: sanitizeInput() removes HTML tags and script content. Strong password policy: Min 8 chars, uppercase, lowercase, number, special char.

doc.title: Epic Technical Specification: Developer Infrastructure
doc.snippet: Unit Testing (Utilities): Coverage Target 100%. Test Pattern: Arrange-Act-Assert. Test Cases: hash.util (hash generation, password comparison), date.util (format, UTC, arithmetic), string.util (slugify, truncate, capitalize), validation.util (phone, password strength, XSS). No mocking required (pure functions).

doc.title: Epics
doc.snippet: As a developer, I want shared utilities and decorators, so that I don't write repetitive code. Technical Notes: @CurrentUser() decorator uses ExecutionContext → request.user extract. Hash util: bcrypt.hash(), bcrypt.compare() wrapper. Pure functions, easily testable. Dependencies: Story 7.1 (i18n).

doc.title: Story 7.1: Internationalization (i18n) Setup
doc.snippet: Testing Infrastructure: Jest unit test structure, TestingModule pattern, Mock services using jest.fn(), Coverage reporting configured. File Structure: Common modules under src/common/, Feature modules under src/modules/, Test files in __tests__/ subdirectories, Barrel exports (index.ts) for clean imports.

artifact.reason: IMPORTANT: @CurrentUser decorator already exists in auth module. Story should MOVE this decorator to src/common/decorators/ for better organization. Current implementation extracts user from request.user (JwtPayload interface). Uses createParamDecorator from @nestjs/common.

IMPORTANT: @Public decorator already exists in auth module. Story should MOVE this decorator to src/common/decorators/ for better organization. Current implementation: SetMetadata(IS_PUBLIC_KEY, true). IS_PUBLIC_KEY constant already defined.

JwtPayload interface used by @CurrentUser decorator. Fields: sub (user ID), phoneNumber, domainID, roles, iat, exp. This interface should be imported and used as return type for @CurrentUser decorator.

JwtAuthGuard already uses IS_PUBLIC_KEY to check for @Public() decorator. Line 8 imports IS_PUBLIC_KEY from auth module. After moving @Public to src/common/decorators/, update import path to: import { IS_PUBLIC_KEY } from '../../common/decorators/public.decorator'.
Existing decorator in src/common/decorators/ for reference. Shows pattern for custom decorators using SetMetadata. Can be used as reference for implementing new decorators.
Existing guard that works with @Permission decorator. Shows how guards interact with custom metadata decorators using Reflector. Reference for understanding decorator-guard integration pattern.

story-context.constraints:
1. CRITICAL: @CurrentUser and @Public decorators already exist in src/modules/auth/decorators/. Story implementation must MOVE these decorators to src/common/decorators/ (not recreate them).
2. After moving decorators, update all import paths: - src/common/guards/jwt-auth.guard.ts: Update IS_PUBLIC_KEY import from auth module to common/decorators - Any controllers using @CurrentUser or @Public: Update imports to common/decorators - Verify no broken imports with: npm run build
3. Pure Function Pattern: All utility functions must be pure (no side effects, stateless, no global state modification).
4. Security Requirements: - bcrypt: Minimum 10 rounds (configurable via BCRYPT_ROUNDS env) - XSS Prevention: sanitizeInput() must remove tags, , and all HTML tags - Password Validation: Min 8 chars, at least 1 uppercase, 1 lowercase, 1 number, 1 special char - Timing Safety: Use bcrypt.compare() for password comparison (prevents timing attacks)
5. Testing Requirements: - 100% unit test coverage for all utility functions (mandatory) - Test files in src/common/utils/__tests__/ - Test pattern: Arrange-Act-Assert - No mocking required for pure functions - Run coverage with: npm run test:cov
6. TypeScript Requirements: - All functions must have TypeScript type definitions - JSDoc comments for all public functions - Export all utilities via index.ts barrel exports
7. @ApiPaginatedResponse Decorator: - @nestjs/swagger not yet installed (Epic 8 dependency) - Decorator should be created but may need @nestjs/swagger package - If package not available, create decorator as stub with TODO comment
8. Barrel Exports: - Create index.ts in src/common/decorators/ exporting all decorators - Create index.ts in src/common/utils/ exporting all utility functions - Pattern: export * from './file-name'
9. Integration with Existing Code: - Hash utilities should be compatible with existing AuthService password handling - @CurrentUser decorator must work with existing JwtStrategy - @Public decorator must work with existing JwtAuthGuard
10. File Naming Convention: - Utilities: kebab-case.util.ts (e.g., hash.util.ts, date.util.ts) - Decorators: kebab-case.decorator.ts (e.g., current-user.decorator.ts) - Tests: kebab-case.spec.ts (e.g., hash.util.spec.ts)

interface.name: JwtPayload
interface.signature: interface JwtPayload { sub: string; // User ID (UUID) phoneNumber: string; // Phone number (E.164 format: +90XXXXXXXXXX) domainID: string; // Domain ID (UUID) roles: string[]; // User roles: ['admin', 'staff', 'manager'] iat: number; // Issued at (Unix timestamp) exp: number; // Expiration (Unix timestamp) }

interface.name: hashPassword
comparePassword
formatDate
interface.signature: export function formatDate(date: Date, format: string): string

interface.name: addDays
interface.signature: export function addDays(date: Date, days: number): Date

interface.name: diffInMs
interface.signature: export function diffInMs(start: Date, end: Date): number

interface.name: truncate
interface.signature: export function truncate(text: string, maxLength: number): string

interface.name: isValidPhone
interface.signature: export function isValidPhone(phone: string, countryCode: string = '+90'): boolean

tests.standards: Jest testing framework with ts-jest for TypeScript support. Test pattern: Arrange-Act-Assert. Unit tests for utilities: 100% coverage target (mandatory). Test files in src/common/utils/__tests__/ directory. Run tests: npm test. Run coverage: npm run test:cov. TestingModule pattern for NestJS testing (decorators). Mock ExecutionContext for decorator tests. No mocking required for pure function utilities. Performance: All utility tests should complete in <100ms total.
tests.locations: - src/common/utils/__tests__/hash.util.spec.ts - src/common/utils/__tests__/date.util.spec.ts - src/common/utils/__tests__/string.util.spec.ts - src/common/utils/__tests__/validation.util.spec.ts - src/common/decorators/__tests__/current-user.decorator.spec.ts - src/common/decorators/__tests__/public.decorator.spec.ts - Integration tests with existing AuthController and AuthService
tests.ideas: AC-7.2.1 (@CurrentUser decorator): - Test: Extract user from request.user using mock ExecutionContext - Test: Returns JwtPayload with correct fields (sub, phoneNumber, domainID, roles) AC-7.2.1 (@Public decorator): - Test: Sets IS_PUBLIC_KEY metadata to true using SetMetadata - Test: JwtAuthGuard bypasses authentication when decorator present AC-7.2.2 (hash.util): - Test: hashPassword() produces valid bcrypt hash (starts with $2b$) - Test: comparePassword() returns true for matching password - Test: comparePassword() returns false for wrong password - Test: Different rounds produce different hashes - Test: BCRYPT_ROUNDS env variable respected AC-7.2.2 (date.util): - Test: formatDate() formats ISO date correctly - Test: toUTC() converts date to UTC timezone - Test: addDays() adds positive days correctly - Test: addDays() subtracts negative days correctly - Test: diffInMs() calculates difference in milliseconds AC-7.2.2 (string.util): - Test: slugify() converts to lowercase with hyphens - Test: slugify() removes special characters - Test: truncate() adds ellipsis when string too long - Test: truncate() returns original if under max length - Test: capitalize() capitalizes first letter only - Test: removeSpecialChars() removes all non-alphanumeric AC-7.2.2 (validation.util): - Test: isValidPhone() accepts +905551234567 format - Test: isValidPhone() rejects invalid formats - Test: isStrongPassword() accepts "Test1234!" (all requirements met) - Test: isStrongPassword() rejects "weak" (too short, no special chars) - Test: isStrongPassword() rejects "NoNumbers!" (no numbers) - Test: sanitizeInput() removes
